Class BudgetManager

java.lang.Object
Controllers.BudgetManager
public class BudgetManager extends Object
Central controller that manages all budget-related operations for the application.

Coordinates between the database layer (CycleDAO, UserDAO), the domain models (BudgetCycle, Expense), and the alert system (AlertManager). Maintains the currently active budget cycle and the logged-in user's PIN in memory for the duration of the session.

  • Constructor Details

    • BudgetManager

      public BudgetManager()
      Constructs a new BudgetManager with fresh DAOs and alert manager.

  • Method Details

    • setCurrentPin

      public void setCurrentPin(String pin)
      Sets the PIN of the currently logged-in user.
      Parameters:
      pin - the user's 4-digit PIN string

    • getCurrentPin

      public String getCurrentPin()
      Returns the PIN of the currently logged-in user.
      Returns:
      the current user's PIN, or null if no user is logged in

    • getAlertManager

      public AlertManager getAlertManager()
      Returns the AlertManager used to trigger budget notifications.
      Returns:
      the alert manager instance

    • registerPin

      public boolean registerPin(String pin)
      Registers a new user PIN in the database.
      Parameters:
      pin - the PIN to register
      Returns:
      true if registration succeeded; false otherwise

    • pinExists

      public boolean pinExists(String pin)
      Checks whether a given PIN is already registered in the database.
      Parameters:
      pin - the PIN to check
      Returns:
      true if the PIN exists; false otherwise

    • savePin

      public void savePin(String pin)
      Saves a PIN by registering it in the database.
      Parameters:
      pin - the PIN to save

    • getSavedPin

      public String getSavedPin()
      Retrieves the first PIN stored in the database.
      Returns:
      the first saved PIN string, or null if none exists

    • loadExistingBudget

      public void loadExistingBudget()
      Loads the most recent budget cycle and its expenses for the current user from the database.

      Does nothing if no user PIN is set. Calculates the remaining balance after loading.

    • getDashboardData

      public DashboardModel getDashboardData()
      Builds and returns the dashboard data model for the current budget cycle.
      Returns:
      a DashboardModel containing summary data, or null if no cycle is active

    • startCycle

      public String startCycle(double allowance, Date start, Date end)
      Creates and saves a new budget cycle for the current user.
      Parameters:
      allowance - the total budget allowance (must be positive)
      start - the start date of the cycle
      end - the end date of the cycle (must not be before start)
      Returns:
      a result string: "success", "no_user", "invalid_allowance", "invalid_dates", or "db_error"

    • getCurrentCycle

      public BudgetCycle getCurrentCycle()
      Returns the currently active budget cycle.
      Returns:
      the current BudgetCycle, or null if none is active

    • getExpenses

      public List<Expense> getExpenses()
      Returns the list of expenses recorded in the current cycle.
      Returns:
      the list of Expense objects

    • getTotalSpent

      public double getTotalSpent()
      Returns the total amount spent in the current cycle.
      Returns:
      the total spent, or 0 if no cycle is active

    • getRemainingBudget

      public double getRemainingBudget()
      Returns the remaining budget for the current cycle after all expenses.
      Returns:
      the remaining balance, or 0 if no cycle is active

    • getFixedDailyLimit

      public double getFixedDailyLimit()
      Returns the fixed daily spending limit based on the total allowance and cycle duration.
      Returns:
      the daily limit amount, or 0.0 if no cycle is active

    • getTodayRemainingDailyLimit

      public double getTodayRemainingDailyLimit()
      Returns the remaining daily budget for today after subtracting today's spending.
      Returns:
      the remaining daily amount (minimum 0)

    • canAddExpense

      public boolean canAddExpense(double amount)
      Checks whether a new expense of the given amount can be added without exceeding any budget limit.
      Parameters:
      amount - the expense amount to check
      Returns:
      true if the expense is within all limits; false otherwise

    • getExpenseRejectionReason

      public String getExpenseRejectionReason(double amount)
      Returns the reason why an expense of the given amount would be rejected, or null if the expense is allowed.
      Parameters:
      amount - the expense amount to evaluate
      Returns:
      "no_cycle" if no cycle is active, "total_budget_exhausted" if the total budget is insufficient, "daily_limit_exceeded" if today's limit would be breached, or null if the expense is allowed

    • getDailySpent

      public double getDailySpent()
      Calculates the total amount spent today across all expenses.
      Returns:
      today's total spending amount

    • getSpentPercentage

      public double getSpentPercentage()
      Returns the percentage of the total allowance that has been spent.
      Returns:
      the spending percentage (0–100), or 0 if no cycle is active

    • getPieChartData

      public Map<String,Double> getPieChartData()
      Aggregates expenses by category name and returns a map suitable for pie chart display.
      Returns:
      a map of category names to their total spending amounts

    • calculateDaysRemaining

      public long calculateDaysRemaining(Date endDate)
      Calculates the number of days remaining until the given end date (inclusive).
      Parameters:
      endDate - the end date of the budget cycle
      Returns:
      the number of days remaining, minimum 1

    • refreshBudgetState

      public void refreshBudgetState()
      Recalculates the remaining balance for the current cycle based on the current expense list.

    • addExpense

      public void addExpense(Expense exp)
      Adds an expense to the in-memory list and triggers budget alert checks.
      Parameters:
      exp - the Expense to add

    • isSameDay

      private boolean isSameDay(Date d1, Date d2)
      Determines whether two dates fall on the same calendar day.
      Parameters:
      d1 - the first date
      d2 - the second date
      Returns:
      true if both dates represent the same day; false otherwise
